MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-28-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT GOULDS PUMPS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
Appearances: Anne T. Acuna of Brayton & Purcell (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and responding party; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 2 and 5 (negligence, strict liability and loss of consortium) and off calendar as to issues 3, 4 and 6 (false representation, civil battery and punitive damages). As to the "OEM" issue, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Bacoka was exposed to products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Bacoka's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar on that issue. As to asbestos content, defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Bradshaw's deposition testimony creates a triable issue whether the products or materials attributable to defendant to which Mr. Bacoka was exposed (packing and gaskets) contained asbestos. Defendant's objections to Mr. Bradshaw's deposition testimony are overruled. Plaintiffs represent that they have dismissed their causes of action for false representation and civil battery and claim for punitive damages. Order signed. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of Aiken Welch, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-20-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FMC CORP.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
Oren P. Noah of Brayton & Purcell LLP (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party; Gavin Whitis of Pond North (217.1240) for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is taken off calendar. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production. Mr. Bacoka's deposition testimony and declaration, Mr. Nykorowytsch's deposition testimony and defendant's responses to General Order No. 129 standard interrogatories create a triable issue whether Mr. Bacoka was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (OEM packing) attributable to defendant. Defendant's moving papers failed to properly make substantial factor causation an issue. Plaintiffs have dismissed their false representation and civil battery causes of action and claim for punitive damages, which were the only issues defendant sought to have summarily adjudicated. Order signed. The court will rule on defendant's evidentiary objections. (503/TLJ) Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of Aiken Welch, CSR #12436

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-14-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FLOWSERVE CORP.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFFS' NEGLIGENCE, STRICT LIABILITY AND LOSS OF CONSORTIUM CAUSES OF ACTION.
Appearances: Jennifer L. Alesio of Brayton & Purcell (415.898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Bacoka was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Bacoka's deposition testimony and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's written contention discovery preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant did not meet its burden of production under the other prong of Aguilar because Mr. Adams' declaration does not affirmatively negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production to make substantial factor causation an issue. Plaintiffs' objections to Ms. Ringo's testimony are sustained. Order signed. (503/TLJ) Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of Aiken Welch, CSR #12436
